metaTitle: Sustainable Weight Management | Piedmont Nutrition Care
metaDescription: Root-cause metabolic repair for weight-loss resistance. GLP-1 medication off-ramp support. Functional nutrition in Winston-Salem, NC.
headline: You've lost the weight before. Your gut is the reason it came back.
subheadline: Weight-loss resistance, yo-yo dieting, and post-GLP-1 weight regain aren't willpower problems. They're gut health problems. When your digestive tract is compromised, your hunger hormones misfire, your metabolism adapts downward, and your body fights to regain every pound you've lost. Piedmont Nutrition Care rebuilds the metabolic foundation that makes weight management sustainable — without starvation, deprivation, or a lifetime prescription.
agitation: You've cut your calories to 1,200 a day. You've done Keto, Whole30, intermittent fasting. You've lost 20 pounds and gained 30 back. Maybe you're on Ozempic and it's working — but you're terrified of what happens when you stop. Maybe you've already stopped and watched the weight return faster than you lost it.

The cycle isn't a character flaw. It's a biological pattern called metabolic adaptation: each round of severe restriction damages your gut microbiome, increases leptin resistance (so your brain never hears the "I'm full" signal), and lowers your resting metabolic rate. You're not failing the diet. The diet is systematically making your biology harder to work with.
howItWorks: Jeanne starts with your metabolic baseline: lab markers, hormonal indicators, gut health assessment, and a full history of what you've tried and how your body responded. She identifies the specific disruptions — leptin resistance, gut dysbiosis, insulin sensitivity, cortisol-driven belly fat accumulation — and builds a food chemistry protocol that repairs each one systematically.

For patients on or coming off GLP-1 medications (Ozempic, Wegovy, Mounjaro): Jeanne provides a "Medication Off-Ramp" protocol that rebuilds the gut flora and metabolic resilience your body needs to maintain results after medication ends. This isn't anti-medication. It's ensuring the medication's results are preserved by a biological foundation, not dependent on a continued prescription.

testimonial.quote: Jeanne is extremely knowledgeable about food, food chemistry, and the latest research findings on nutrition. She can suggest dietary changes to prevent cancer, boost a sluggish metabolism, improve blood sugar, or lower LDL.
testimonial.author: Liz

ctaTriggers: Stop fighting your biology. Start repairing it.
